title: Telling time on a clock
description: Tell time on labeled analog clocks to the nearest 5 minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the time on the clock.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5d32e44363943ee9b3afd9d59814493febca6433)

**What time is it?**

The time is [[☃ numeric-input 1]] : [[☃ numeric-input 2]].
hints:
On a clock there are two hands. The $\redD{\text{hour hand}}$ is the shorter hand.  The $\blueE{\text{minute hand}}$ is the longer hand.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45a764740b107b33cf6707c08fe70c7abdbda8c9)

The hour hand is between $2$ and $3$, so it is after $2$ o'clock but not yet $3$ o'clock.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1578481444e74141bc216263ebb7bc419500dbf6)

The hour is $2$.

The minute hand starts pointing straight up for $0$ minutes.  

When counting minutes, the distance between each tick mark represents $1$ minute.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1ab12f55cf150cf2bc6ccbb9f8bee6c187fafa8e)

Each number on the clock represents $5$ minutes, with a total of $\blueE{60}$ minutes in each hour.

We can relate the clock to a number line. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1460e3ba775a51c71f76cb4128ec5da367e821b1)

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e22077dbdc544647afa8f87967d479d0cda6be4e)

The minute hand is at $5$ minutes past $2{:}00$.
The time is $2{:}05$.

The time is $12{:}00$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which clock matches the time shown on the number line?**

![The number line showing minute marks goes from 1:00 to 2:00 with labels every 15 minutes. Point A is labeled at 10 minutes past the 1:30 label.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd33ad1b3949f2e0eb3b2f6320e3c83e6a6d9e9c)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The $\goldD{A}$ is at $\redD1{:}{\blueE{40}}$ because it is located $\blueE{40}$ ticks after $\redD1{:}{00}$.

On a clock, $\redD1{:}{00} $ looks like this:

![A clock shows the short hour hand points to 1. The long minute hand points to 12. Labels outside the edge of the clock are number 1 through 12. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/485f89cacc5c57ac3f80916ece9755fbf0cacaab)

And just like on the number line, $1{:}{\blueE{40}}$ is $\blueE{40}$ ticks after $1{:}{00}.$

![A clock shows the short hour hand is halfway between 1 and 2. The long minute hand points to 8. Outside the edge of the clock 12 is labeled 0, 1 is labeled 5, 2 is labeled 10, and so on with 11 labeled 55.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c6020e52d3d31c8626202fb1ec77afc1863e12ae)

This clock shows $1{:}{40}$.

![A clock shows the short hour hand is halfway between 1 and 2. The long minute hand points to 8.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f3f2267a63e152db5b75979365c16297a898f92c)

choices.content: ![A clock shows the short hour hand is halfway between 1 and 2. The long minute hand points to 6.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0e1ac8003470314ad7922dbb673bda6f81765c7a)
![A clock shows the short hour hand is halfway between 1 and 2. The long minute hand points to 4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8c1f3033af49b152fa8144d90daf4673ecb996b0)
![A clock shows the short hour hand is halfway between 1 and 2. The long minute hand points to 8.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f3f2267a63e152db5b75979365c16297a898f92c)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which clock shows $4{:}55$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
On a clock there are two hands. The $\redD{\text{hour hand}}$ is the shorter hand.  The $\blueE{\text{minute hand}}$ is the longer hand.

![A clock shows the short hour hand is at 5. The long minute hand points to 12.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45a764740b107b33cf6707c08fe70c7abdbda8c9)

So, $\redD{4}{:}{00} $ looks like this:

![A clock shows the short hour hand is at 4. The long minute hand points to 12. Labels outside the edge of the clock are number 1 through 12.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2ed7caa12c2157999185ab570034395a4b2fc3dc)

$\redD{4}{:}{\blueE{55}}$ is $\blueE{55}$ ticks from $\redD{4}{:}{00}.$

![A clock shows the short hour hand is at 11. The long minute hand points to 11. Outside the edge of the clock 12 is labeled 0, 1 is labeled 5, 2 is labeled 10, and so on with 11 labeled 55.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c7c4b8ad9d05de61d68f32596a19ffa6051fdc9b)

Notice the hour hand is past $4{:}00$ but not quite to $5{:}00$.

This clock shows $4{:}{55}$.

![A clock shows the short hour hand is at 5. The long minute hand points to 11.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c5a9ae7aee11127bfd5b6fe190ebab7264de7290)

choices.content: ![A clock shows the short hour hand is at 5. The long minute hand points to 11.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c5a9ae7aee11127bfd5b6fe190ebab7264de7290)
![A clock shows the short hour hand is between 3 and 4. The long minute hand points to 11.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ae08c704aea1021846c87c8e5c8199839b1ea394)
![A clock shows the short hour hand is just past 4. The long minute hand points to 5.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/75374464a07e5af216016119254c54fc43199f67)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which clock shows $8{:}15$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
On a clock there are two hands. The $\redD{\text{hour hand}}$ is the shorter hand.  The $\blueE{\text{minute hand}}$ is the longer hand.

![A clock shows the short hour hand points to 5. The long minute hand points to 12.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45a764740b107b33cf6707c08fe70c7abdbda8c9)

So, $\redD{8}{:}{00} $ looks like this:

![A clock shows the short hour hand points to 8. The long minute hand points to 12. Labels outside the edge of the clock are number 1 through 12.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f87cde384d3ce378a889e21437bbafe2440e8fd7)

$\redD{8}{:}{\blueE{15}}$ is $\blueE{15}$ ticks from $\redD{8}{:}{00}.$

![A clock shows the short hour hand is just past 5. The long minute hand points to 1. Outside the edge of the clock 12 is labeled 0, 1 is labeled 5, 2 is labeled 10, and so on with 11 labeled 55. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32c53c89a80267888cf320b6c3a9d78f70666a4d)

$\redD{1}{:}{\blueE{25}}$ is $\blueE{25}$ ticks from $\redD{1}{:}{00}.$

![A clock shows the short hour hand is past 1. The long minute hand points to 5. Outside the edge of the clock 12 is labeled 0, 1 is labeled 5, 2 is labeled 10, and so on with 11 labeled 55.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/43fa4517a5fff374006a7c1e0bef6da4c79e4889)

Notice the hour hand is past $1{:}00$ but not quite to $2{:}00$.

$\redD{5}{:}{\blueE{25}}$ is $\blueE{25}$ ticks from $\redD{5}{:}{00}.$

![A clock shows the short hour hand is past 5. The long minute hand points to 5. Outside the edge of the clock 12 is labeled 0, 1 is labeled 5, 2 is labeled 10, and so on with 11 labeled 55. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7d24f93ae1e01e3b59e5a3fc637613411dc524b3)

Notice the hour hand is past $5{:}00$ but not quite to $6{:}00$.
